concerns.text: FR-005 mandates a sensitivity analysis sweeping thresholds over the set {0.01, 0.05, 0.1}. However, User Story 3 (US-3) and SC-004 describe the sweep as evaluating 'relative to the standard error' (e.g., 0.01 * SE). FR-005 omits the 'times the standard error' qualifier, creating a potential ambiguity where the system might sweep absolute values (0.01, 0.05, 0.1) instead of relative multipliers. This contradicts the intent in US-3 and SC-004.

The 'Assumption about threshold justification' section repeats the specific set {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} as 'community standards'. While not a direct contradiction, hardcoding specific numerical thresholds in the 'Assumptions' section (which should be qualitative constraints) conflicts with the 'Planning docs state *what* will be measured... defer specific empirical values' guidance in the SC-004 header. The specific values should be moved to the implementation plan or clearly marked as 'proposed' rather than 'assumed' facts.
